posts.caption:
It’s been a little over a month since #GoodDamage landed in the wild. 

But somethings missing, and that something was just delivered…

What is it? You gotta watch to find out. Maybe it’s another book..

I’m so excited and grateful for the reception of GD - and cannot wait to share a few things that are in the pipeline…until then, thank you thank you thank you. 

Did you read + review Good Damage? 🔗 in bio 

#lgbt #memoir #bookstagram

“Everybody Knew That We Had Too Much Fun”. 

If you know us, then you know I didn’t bury the lead…29 years of having too much fun is our DNA. I wrote this chapter in #GoodDamage because without Jessica’s stubborn belief in me…I don’t know that I would have made it out of my 20s (in one piece 😳.)

Here’s to 40 more years of craziness, milestones and absolutely crushing life 🤘🏻. 

If you haven’t yet - check out the hottest summer read: Good Damage: Tragedy. Lightly Polished, with a Side of Optimism is available everywhere books are sold 🔗 in bio. 

#newbook #friendship #memoir

For every bit of self-doubt, I got something back I didn’t expect, people telling me #GoodDamage helped them.

Good Damage took two and a half years to write. Turns out I’d been working on it for decades, I just didn’t know that’s what I was doing. 

Never give up 🤘🏻❤️🙏🏻

Available everywhere books are sold. 🔗 in bio. Did you order + review? 

#lgbt #bookish #summerreading  @artsatlanta @ripplesmediabooks

When @artsatlanta s Reid Davis sat down to discuss #GoodDamage with me, I felt seen in a way that I wasn’t expecting. He landed on exactly the thing I was nervous people would flatten this into – a coming out story with grief sprinkled on top. Coming out is just one of the things that happened while I was busy surviving everything else. And I am proud of who I am, but like you...I am so much more than any one-dimensional label can cover.

“The June 2026 release of Toler’s memoir may coincide with Pride celebrations, but his story is far more than a coming-out tale. Good Damage has wide appeal not just for its relatability, but because — like its author — it’s never boring. The real-life tale is funny, keenly observed, and inspirational.”

“The book was born from a desire to help people, says Toler, who endured a childhood that forced him to grow up quickly.”

Nail on the head..

He also got into the parts I don’t usually lead with in interviews, the voice memos I dictated instead of typing because typing made me too careful, the 18 months it took to turn a stream of consciousness into something with a spine, and the vulture-versus-hummingbird perspective that inspired the back half of the book. 

🔗 in bio. 

Read this first, then read the book. It’ll make more sense why I wrote it the way I did. 

Good Damage summer is here. Did you order + review? 

#memoir #indieauthorsofinstagram #summerreads

22 days ago, I published my debut memoir, #GoodDamage. Nothing else I’ve done compares to writing a book. I didn’t plan most of it, I just start talking into a voice memo, and the words came out before I could second-guess them. The manuscript started that way, before it was ever typed. 

It’s also the first time I’ve let people see the dark spots without immediately covering them with a joke.

Being vulnerable, saying things that I “probably shouldn’t say” - none of that makes me uncomfortable. I’ve operated Ted that way my whole life, and it’s the same instinct that made me decent on a stage.

What I hadn’t figured out, was the part where the story left the room without me. No tone, no face, and no context built-in - just the book, doing whatever it wants and someone else else’s head. (which almost made my head explode.)🤯 

It wasn’t a fear of what people think of me. I can manage that, and I’ve made peace with it. I’m afraid of being misread. If you read good damage and walk away with thoughts about me, fine. But if you walk away with the wrong idea of where my heart sits - that’s the one thing I wanted to get right. I know that’s not fully possible, but I still want it.

Needless to say, when my publisher suggested submitting good damage for reviews, the idea of a stranger reading my book stopped being theoretical and was immediately followed by “holy shit what if they think I’m a trash writer?” “what if I turned something in that wasn’t 100% perfect yet?” “ what if…what if…what if”. 

@kirkus_reviews was the one review that got into my head. A quick search on “Are Kirkus reviews brutal?” Will tell you what you’re dealing with. “ Yes, Kirkus reviews has a long-standing reputation for being notoriously, tough and uncompromising”. High industry, standards, and no particular interest in your feelings about them.😭😳

Not the best search to run eight minutes after I already hit submit, and there was nothing left to do but wait. 

I received the review from Kirkus yesterday. 

Their Verdict: Get it. 

What are you waiting for? Good Damage is available everywhere books are sold. 🔗 bio

Grab your copy and don’t forget to review!! #indieauthor #memoir

There’s a chapter in #GoodDamage called The Space Between Us Isn’t That Wide.

It’s about the parts of you no algorithm can sort: not your zip code, not your bank account, not who you pray to, not who you love. None of it steps in when life drops you to your knees. None of it blocks the blow.

Strip away the labels we hold onto so tight, and what’s left is the place we meet.

That’s where we’re the same. 

Good Damage is available everywhere books are sold! 🔗in bio. 

Support locally in ATL @littleshopofstories @vahibooks 

Your #summerreading thanks you - GD is also perfect for your next #bookclub read.  #indieauthor

Stoked to receive positive early feedback on #gooddamage 

Clarion gave Good Damage 4 out of 5 writing: “Leaning into the power of human connections, Trey Toler’s laugh-out-loud memoir Good Damage looks back on discovering a culture and a vocation where his complicated puzzle pieces fit together.“ 

It’s a strange (but beautiful) feeling to send something so personal out and into the world, and then read how others connected to your story. 

I’m grateful for every person who’s invested time in reading GD, reviewed GD, shared a pic with GD, traveled with GD. My heart is full. 

Reviews are HUGE. Hope these help inspire your review ❤️. What did YOU think?

Order Good Damage today! 🔗 in bio it’s a perfect #beachread and you’ll love it by the pool or where your #summerreading takes you. #memoir

Oh we’re just getting started!

#gooddamage summer is here, and what better way to heal, grow, live…than with laughter. 

EVERYONE said the same thing when I told them I wrote a book…

Can you guess what that was?

PS: let me rock your book club 🤘🏻.

“The Monsters We Learn To Name” is a chapter about the things you carry so long, you forget you’re carrying them.

One of mine lived deep in the back of a closet. Late 90s. The South. Winter coats nobody wore, as my only witnesses.

I won’t pretend it wasn’t hard, it was… but it also taught me when you spend years reading the room just to survive it, you get very good at noticing what people leave out of their own sentences.

The thing I called a monster ended up being the reason I can connect with pretty much anyone, which is elite character development, lol. I wouldn’t trade it. #gooddamage #memoir #summerreading 

Good Damage is available everywhere books are sold. Did you order and review? 🔗 in bio
